LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS THOMAS HORMEL AND THOMAS D. HORMEL TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUDULENT TRANSFER. CC 3439.04.(2)(A)&(B) PROVIDE THAT THE FRAUDULENT TRANSFER CAUSE OF ACTION CAN BE ESTABLISHED WHERE THE DEBTOR IS INSOLVENT. PLAINTIFF FAILES TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING THE LIMITED PARTNERSHIP'S INSOLVENCY AND THE TESTIMONY OF MS. LEGG IS INADMISSIBLE UNDER EVID. CODE SECTION 1292. THE PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE DERIVATIVE CLAIMS OF CONSPIRACY AND CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH THE BREACH OF CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFF PRESENTS NO EVIDENCE SHOWING ALTER EGO LIABILITY OR THAT DEFENDANTS ACTED AS GENERAL PARTNERS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047.(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS WILL STOREY AND VERLE STOREY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON WEBB'S FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO ADD THE STOREYS AS JUDGMENT DEBTORS AND EVIDENCE IS GRANTED. DEFENDANTS WILLIAM STOREY AND VERLE STOREY HAVE MET THEIR INITIAL BURDEN. DEFENDANTS CANNOT BE ADDED TO THE PRIOR JUDGMENT AS ALTER EGOS BECAUSE THEY DID NOT CONTROL THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION-INDEED THEY HAD NO KNOWLEDGE OF IT UNTIL THE FILING OF THIS SUBSEQUENT ACTION. (NEC ELECTRONICS INC. V. HURT (1989) 208 CAL.APP.3D 772, 781. SEE UMF NO. 5.) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT MET HER SHIFTING BURDEN. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT THAT SHE NEED NOT SHOW CONTROL OF THE UNDERLYING LITIGATION WHEN A SUBSEQUENT ACTION IS FILED IS NOT SUPPORTED BY THE LEGAL AUTHORITY CITED. " ' "[A] POINT WHICH IS MERELY SUGGESTED BY [A PARTY'S] COUNSEL, WITH NO SUPPORTING ARGUMENT OR AUTHORITY, IS DEEMED TO BE WITHOUT FOUNDATION AND REQUIRES NO DISCUSSION." [CITATION.]' [CITATION.]" (DO IT URSELF MOVING & STORAGE, INC. V. BROWN, LEIFER, SLATKIN & BERNS (1992) 7 CAL.APP.4TH 27, 35, SUPERSEDED BY STATUTE ON OTHER GROUNDS IN UNION BANK V. SUP. CT. (1995) 31 CAL.APP.4TH 573, 582.) THE COURT SIGNED THE PROPOSED ORDER THAT WAS SENT BY EMAIL TO THE COURT. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: MARIA VISCIO, CSR# 12353.
DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT TAYLOR, WILLIAM H TAYLOR, PATRICVIA

JOINT REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT STOREY, WILL M STOREY, VERLE
DECLARATION OF GILDA R. TURITZJIN SUPPORT OF REPLY TO OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT STOREY, WILL M STOREY, VERLE

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE MILLER DISCLOSED AT THE HEARING THAT ELLEN LIU WAS A LEGAL RESEARCH ATTORNEY AT THE COURT APPROXIMATELY 3 YEARS AGO, AND SHE WORKED WITH HER FROM TIME TO TIME. SINCE THEN THE JUDGE HAVE SEEN HER ON A FEW OCCASSIONS AT SOCIAL EVENTS TO WHICH THEY HAVE BOTH BEEN INVITED. JUDGE MILLER IS ACQUAINTED WITH GILDA TURITZ THROUGH BAR ACTIVITIES OVER THE YEARS AND ONCE BRIEFLY WAS AN OPPOSING COUNSEL ON A CASE WHERE SHE WAS AN ATTORNEY ABOUT 20 YEARS AGO. ABOUT 4 MONTHS AGO, SHE ADMINISTERED THE OATH TO SWEAR MS. TURITZ'S DAUGHTER-IN-LAW INTO THE STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA IN HER COURTROOM. SHE RUN INTO MS. TURITZ OCCASIONALLY FROM TIME TO TIME IN PROFESSIONAL OR BAR ASSOCIATION LIKE SETTINGS. NONE OF WHAT THE JUDGE DISCLOSED AS TO MS. LIU AND MS. TURITZ AFFECTS HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL IN DECIDING THE MOTION INFRONT OF HER AND DOES NOT BELIEVE THAT A PERSON AWARE OF THE FACTS WOULD RESONABLY BELIEVE THAT SHE COULD NOT BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL THE OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTIONS. NO QUESTIONS WERE ASKED OR OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS WILL STOREY AND VERLE STOREY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. (SEE MINI-MUNUTES FOR DETAILS) PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: ANN DELGADO, CSR # 11185, TEL. NO. (951)764-4047. (302/MJM)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANTS WILL STOREY and VERLE STOREY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Off calendar. Duplicative entry. =302/MJM

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudciation
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JORDAN A. SUSSMAN OF THE ZIEF, DIMENT & GLICKMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 986-3644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GILDA R. TURITZ OF SIDEMAN & BANCROFT LLP, TEL. NO. 733-3920 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT THOMAS HORMEL & THOMAS HORMEL TRUST;
ATTY. ADAM M. STARR OF THE LAW FIRM OF MARKOWITZ HERBOLD & MEHLHAF, P.C., TEL. NO. (503)-295-3085 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS WILL M. STOREY AND VERLE STOREY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that Ellen Liu was a legal research attorney at the Court approximately 3 years ago, and she worked with her from time to time. Since then the judge have seen her on a few occassions at social events to which they have both been invited. Judge Miller is acquainted with Gilda Turitz through bar activities over the years and once briefly was an opposing counsel on a case where she was an attorney about 20 years ago. About 4 months ago, she administered the oath to swear Ms. Turitz's daughter-in-
law into the State Bar of California in her courtroom. She run into Ms. Turitz occasionally from time to time in professional or bar association like settings. None of what the judge disclosed as to Ms. Liu and Ms. Turitz affects her ability to be fair and impartial in deciding the motion infront of her and does not believe that a person aware of the facts would resonably believe that she could not be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANTS THOMAS HORMEL and THOMAS D. HORMEL TRUST'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the 1st cause of action for fraudulent transfer. CC 3439.04.(2)(A)&(B) provide that the fraudulent transfer cause of action can be established where the debtor is insolvent. Plaintiff failes to create a triable issue of material fact regarding the limited partnership's insolvency and the testimony of Ms. Legg is inadmissible under Evid. Code section 1292. The plaintiff cannot establish the derivative claims of conspiracy and constructive trust. Plaintiff cannot establish the breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff presents no evidence showing alter ego liability or that defendants acted as general partners. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047.=302/mjm

AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Motion For Summary Judgment Filed By Storey, Will M
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. JORDAN A. SUSSMAN OF THE ZIEF, DIMENT & GLICKMAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 986-3644 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. GILDA R. TURITZ OF SIDEMAN & BANCROFT LLP, TEL. NO. 733-3920 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT THOMAS HORMEL & THOMAS HORMEL TRUST;
ATTY. ADAM M. STARR OF THE LAW FIRM OF MARKOWITZ HERBOLD & MEHLHAF, P.C., TEL. NO. (503)-295-3085 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS WILL M. STOREY AND VERLE STOREY BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - Judge Miller disclosed at the hearing that Ellen Liu was a legal research attorney at the Court approximately 3 years ago, and she worked with her from time to time. Since then the judge have seen her on a few occassions at social events to which they have both been invited. Judge Miller is acquainted with Gilda Turitz through bar activities over the years and once briefly was an opposing counsel on a case where she was an attorney about 20 years ago. About 4 months ago, she administered the oath to swear Ms. Turitz's daughter-in-
law into the State Bar of California in her courtroom. She run into Ms. Turitz occasionally from time to time in professional or bar association like settings. None of what the judge disclosed as to Ms. Liu and Ms. Turitz affects her ability to be fair and impartial in deciding the motion infront of her and does not believe that a person aware of the facts would resonably believe that she could not be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel the opportunity to ask questions. No questions were asked or objections were raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANTS WILL STOREY and VERLE STOREY'S AMENDED MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or, in the alternative, Motion For Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff cannot establish the 1st cause of action for fraudulent transfer. CC 3439.04.(2)(A)&(B) provide that the fraudulent transfer cause of action can be established where the debtor is insolvent. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of material fact regarding the limited partnership's insolvency and the testimony of Ms. Legg is inadmissinle under Evid. Code section 1292. The plaintiff cannot establish the derivative claims of conspiracy and constructive trust. Plaintiff cannot establish the breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff presents no evidence showing alter ego liability or that defendants acted as general partners. Evidentiary Objections were overruled. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437C(g) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Ann Delgado, CSR # 11185, Tel. No. (951)764-4047.

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-03-2014 Continued To Jun-10-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Webbs Fourth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief To Amend Judgment To Add The Storey'S As Judgment Debtors And Evidence
Off Calendar Mar-03-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was uncontested. DEFENDANTS WILL STOREY and VERLE STOREY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION On Webb's Fourth Cause Of Action For Declaratory Relief To Amend Judgment To Add The Storeys As Judgment Debtors And Evidence is Granted. Defendants William Storey and Verle Storey have met their initial burden. Defendants cannot be added to the prior judgment as alter egos because they did not control the underlying litigation-indeed they had no knowledge of it until the filing of this subsequent action. (NEC Electronics Inc. v. Hurt (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 772, 781. See UMF no. 5.) Plaintiff has not met her shifting burden. Plaintiff's argument that she need not show control of the underlying litigation when a subsequent action is filed is not supported by the legal authority cited. " ' "[A] point which is merely suggested by [a party's] counsel, with no supporting argument or authority, is deemed to be without foundation and requires no discussion." [Citation.]' [Citation.]" (Do It Urself Moving & Storage, Inc. v. Brown, Leifer, Slatkin & Berns (1992) 7 Cal.App.4th 27, 35, superseded by statute on other grounds in Union Bank v. Sup. Ct. (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 573, 582.) The Court signed the proposed order that was sent by email to the court. Judge: Peter Busch; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Viscio, CSR# 12353.
